headline: NYSE Owner Moves Deeper Into Crypto. This Top Fund Manager Is Bullish on Its Stock
summary: Intercontinental Exchange is investing in crypto exchange OKX at a $25 billion valuation. Barron’s Roundtable member Todd Ahlsten of Parnassus likes the stock because of its diversification efforts.

headline: A Look At Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) Valuation After Record Volumes And Blockchain Settlement Launch
summary: Why February’s volume spike matters for Intercontinental Exchange stock Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) just reported February trading statistics showing a 17% year-over-year jump in average daily volume and record open interest, following record 2025 earnings and the launch of a blockchain-based settlement platform. See our latest analysis for Intercontinental Exchange. After a six day winning streak tied to record 2025 results and the blockchain settlement launch, ICE’s share price has...

headline: NYSE Owner Invests in Crypto Exchange OKX
summary: ICE will license OKX's spot crypto prices and launch U.S.-regulated futures contracts tied to them.  Subject to regulatory approval, ICE’s U.S. futures and NYSE’s tokenized equities will be available on OKX's platform, which serves more than 120 million customers.  Terms of the minority investment weren’t disclosed.

headline: Intercontinental Exchange Faces Scrutiny Over Prediction Markets And Valuation Concerns
summary: Intercontinental Exchange backed prediction markets, including Polymarket and Kalshi, face scrutiny over contracts tied to sensitive geopolitical outcomes. Regulators and critics raised concerns about contracts involving potential violent events, including markets on the fate of Iran's Ayatollah Khamenei. Kalshi halted trading in the disputed contracts and reimbursed users after controversy around the ethical and regulatory boundaries of such bets. For you as an investor watching NYSE:ICE,...

headline: Sen. Merkley proposes prediction market ban for government officials after Maduro, Iran bets
summary: After a series of dubious bets placed on the ouster of Venezuela's Maduro and Iran strikes, lawmakers are looking to ban insider trading on prediction markets.
